label.purpose: Small status chips like the 'Live experiment' badge.

code.purpose: Inline code and code blocks; ligatures disabled.

shadows.name: panel-ambient
shadows.purpose: Quiet lift under the live demo card; deepens in dark mode.

shadows.name: button-lift
shadows.purpose: Colored low-opacity glow under primary CTAs only.

motion.name: ease-out-quart
motion.purpose: Default easing for hover, focus, and the hero rise entrance.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary CTA: forest-green fill, white text, colored ambient lift.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: 'Lexend Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.02rem; color: #fff; background: #00683e; border: 1px solid #00683e; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.8rem 1.6rem; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 10px 24px -12px #00683e; transition: background 130ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), transform 130ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #006037; transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f18426; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Lower-emphasis action: transparent fill, green text, hairline border.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; font-family: 'Lexend Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.02rem; color: #00683e; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #d4ddd7; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.8rem 1.6rem; text-decoration: none; transition: border-color 130ms ease, background 130ms ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #00683e; background: #eef5f0; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #f18426; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Live Experiment Badge
components.description: Signature status chip: pulsing coral dot + green label on the demo panel.
components.css: .ds-live-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.45rem; font-family: 'Lexend Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.01em; color: #00683e; background: rgba(0,104,62,0.08); border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.2rem 0.6rem 0.2rem 0.55rem; } .ds-live-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 50%; background: #db3424; box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(219,52,36,0.55); animation: ds-pulse 2s ease-out infinite; } @keyframes ds-pulse { 0% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 0 rgba(219,52,36,0.55); } 70%,100% { box-shadow: 0 0 0 6px transparent; } }

components.name: Demo Panel
components.description: Elevated surface card that houses the live jsPsych experiment.
components.css: .ds-panel { margin: 0; padding: 1.75rem; background: #f4f8f5; border: 1px solid #e1e8e3; border-radius: 16px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.04), 0 12px 28px -18px rgba(0,0,0,0.25); } .ds-panel-title { display: block; font-family: 'Lexend Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.5rem; letter-spacing: -0.01em; color: #15201a; } .ds-panel-lead { margin: 0.4rem 0 0; font-family: 'Lexend Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #5e6561; }

components.name: Directory Link Row
components.description: Ecosystem 'Find your way around' link: bold green title with orange arrow, muted descriptor, hairline divider.
components.html: Reference→The complete API for the core library, plugins, and extensions.
components.css: .ds-path { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.3rem; padding: 1.15rem 0; border-bottom: 1px solid #e1e8e3; text-decoration: none; } .ds-path-title { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.4rem; font-family: 'Lexend Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.15rem; color: #15201a; transition: color 120ms ease; } .ds-path-arrow { color: #f18426; transition: transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-path:hover .ds-path-title { color: #00683e; } .ds-path:hover .ds-path-arrow { transform: translateX(4px); } .ds-path-desc { font-family: 'Lexend Variable', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #5e6561; }

components.name: Inline Code
components.description: Inline code token: JetBrains Mono on a green-tinted surface.
components.css: .ds-code { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.95em; font-variant-ligatures: none; background: #eef5f0; color: #1f2622; padding: 0.1em 0.4em; border-radius: 6px; }

narrative.overview: jsPsych is open-source software for building behavioral experiments, and its documentation should feel like the workbench where those experiments get built: a well-lit, well-organized surface where the tools are within reach and the work itself is on display. The signature move is showing, not telling - the homepage runs a real jsPsych experiment in front of you rather than describing one. Every surface is content-first and quietly confident: the framework's identity (forest green, leaf green, orange, coral) supplies the warmth while the layout gets out of the way. The personality is scientific and approachable, and the site is a hub for an entire ecosystem of packages, so coherence across many surfaces matters more than per-page flourish. Restraint is the discipline: one green carries the brand, orange is a rare accent, and generous whitespace plus a reading-tuned typeface (Lexend) do the heavy lifting.
keyCharacteristics: Live, runnable demos as first-class content - show, don't tell.
One forest-green brand voice; orange as a <=10% accent, never decoration.
Reading-tuned humanist type (Lexend) + clear monospace (JetBrains Mono) for code.
Green-tinted neutrals, never warm cream; near-black-with-a-pine-tint in dark mode.
WCAG 2.1 AA throughout; meaning never rests on color alone (red/green safe).

rules.name: The One-Green Rule
rules.body: Forest green is the only color that carries structure. If a second hue is doing structural work on a screen, one of them is wrong.

rules.name: The Orange-Is-Rare Rule
rules.body: Orange appears on <=10% of any screen - accents, focus, selection, one flourish. Its scarcity is the point.

rules.name: The No-Cream Rule
rules.body: Neutrals tint toward the brand green, never toward warm beige/sand/parchment. Warmth comes from the orange accent and the type.

rules.name: The Color-Is-Never-Alone Rule
rules.body: Status, feedback, and diffs must always carry an icon, label, or shape in addition to color. Color-blind readers must lose nothing.

rules.name: The Tighten-Only-Large Rule
rules.body: Negative letter-spacing applies only to display/h1/h2. Body and small headings keep default tracking.

rules.name: The Depth-From-Tone Rule
rules.body: In dark mode, elevation is lightness, never shadow. Step the surface tone instead of adding a drop shadow.

dos: Do let forest green carry the brand and reserve Signal Orange for accents, focus rings, selection, and one flourish - <=10% of any screen.
Do keep body text at Pine Ink / Moonlit Ink (15:1 / 14:1) and never let secondary text go lighter than Slate Sage (#5e6561, 6:1).
Do tint neutrals toward the brand green; carry warmth in the accent and type.
Do pair every status/feedback/diff color with an icon, label, or shape - the palette leans red/green.
Do lead with a runnable demo or real code where you'd otherwise write a paragraph.
Do cap prose at 65-75ch and disable ligatures in code so exact characters read true.
Do convey depth in dark mode by stepping surface lightness, not by adding shadows.
donts: Don't ship the generic Docusaurus default look - uncustomized green/blue, default footer, stock template cards.
Don't drift corporate / SaaS-y: no gradient heroes, marketing buzzwords, or enterprise-cold polish.
Don't make surfaces cluttered or busy - no rainbow of admonitions, no decoration competing with content.
Don't go sterile or intimidating with cold walls of reference-only text.
Don't use a warm cream/sand/beige background, or token names like --paper/--cream; neutrals lean green.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't use gradient text, decorative glassmorphism, or hard 2014-style drop shadows.
Don't color orange as body text; use Signal Orange Ink (#b55800) only when orange text is unavoidable.
